wpm.label: Speaking rate
wpm.feedback: Very slow (95 wpm) — pick up the pace to maintain audience engagement.

pitch_variation.label: Pitch variation
pitch_variation.feedback: Limited pitch variation (15.4 Hz) — add more rises/falls for emphasis.

energy_variation.label: Energy variation
energy_variation.feedback: Some energy variation (0.0156) — add more deliberate emphasis.

pause_ratio.label: Pause ratio
pause_ratio.feedback: Almost no pauses (2.9%) — use silence strategically for impact.

vocal_emotion.label: Vocal emotion (Neutral)
vocal_emotion.feedback: Neutral vocal tone — consider adding more warmth and enthusiasm.

mismatches.observed_tone: High energy with strong pitch variation
mismatches.expected_tone: Balanced, confident, and slightly subdued to match serious leadership content
mismatches.reason: The speaker’s energetic delivery may overwhelm the audience when discussing complex leadership concepts, making it harder to absorb key points

coaching_tips: Use a steadier pitch by consciously lowering the variation in the lower register, especially when outlining core leadership principles;
Introduce deliberate pauses after each major point to give the audience time to process the information;
Match the energy level to the content—lower the intensity during explanations of strategic frameworks and raise it when sharing success stories;
Practice a neutral emotional tone in rehearsal to avoid sounding overly enthusiastic, ensuring the message of leadership seriousness is conveyed;
Record and review a short segment to monitor pitch and energy, adjusting as needed to maintain a professional, engaging delivery.
